events.title: Other Worlds: Online Speculative Fiction Book Club
events.description: TPL'S ONLINE SPECULATIVE FICTION BOOK GROUP! Regularly meets the last Wednesday of each month via Zoom to discuss intriguing, mind-expanding books from multiple genres. Register today and join us online. May 2026 Selection: How to Become the Dark Lord and Die Trying by Django Wexler Groundhog Day meets Guardians of the Galaxy in Django Wexler’s laugh-out-loud fantasy tale about a young woman who, tired of defending humanity from the Dark Lord, decides to become the Dark Lord herself.Davi has done this all before. She’s tried to be the hero and take down the all-powerful Dark Lord. A hundred times she’s rallied humanity and made the final charge. But the time loop always gets her in the end. Sometimes she’s killed quickly. Sometimes it takes a while. But she’s been defeated every time.This time? She’s done being the hero and done being stuck in this endless time loop. If the Dark Lord always wins, then maybe that’s who she needs to be. It’s Davi’s turn to play on the winning side. Book one of the Dark Lord Davi series from a PNW author. How to Become the Dark Lord and Die Trying Speculative Fiction? What's that? Do you like science fiction? That's speculative fiction. Fantasy? OK, that's speculative fiction too. Horror, magical realism, alternate history, slipstream, steampunk, cyberpunk, dystopias, apocalyptic tales, and stories that are just plain weird? Yep. Those are all eligible for our speculative fiction menu!If reading outside the lines of everyday life interests you, we invite you to join our online community as we explore our reading horizons. Other Worlds might be the change of pace you're looking for.

events.title: High Intermediate - Advanced ESL Book Club - A Farewell to Arms
events.description: ESL Book Club will read A Farewell to Arms by Ernest Hemingway. This book club is best for high intermediate - advanced English learners. Students will read at home each week. Then you will meet as a group once a week to talk about the story, learn new vocabulary, and practice conversation. Book club will be led by Fernando Lopez. Meeting dates: May 28; June 4, 11, 18, 25; July 2, 9, 16.Meeting time: Thursdays, 10:30am - 12:00nRegistration is open now! Book club is limited to 8 participants. Books will be provided for registered participants to keep. Please consider your schedule and do not register if you will miss more than one meeting.A Farewell to Arms:Hemingway’s masterpiece is not only one of the best novels we have about World War I but is also a tender, haunting love story. Ernest Hemingway drew from his own war experiences when he crafted this remarkable story of an American ambulance driver serving on the Italian front and his love for a beautiful English nurse. The novel, Hemingway’s first best seller, is marked by vivid depictions of the horrors of the battlefield—but also by the heartrending vicissitudes of a passionate affair of the heart between his protagonists, Frederic and Catherine, leading up to a tragic ending that is all the more powerful for its famously understated expression.

events.title: Book Clubs of EPL: The Five
events.description: Love reading? Love to talk about books you've read? Come join us for lively discussions on all types of fiction and non-fiction. We'll feature different titles and authors each month. Come and share your impressions, thoughts and interpretations of a book we have all read. This month we will be reading The Five: the Untold Lives of the Women Killed by Jack the Ripper by Hallie Rubenhold.Five devastating human stories and a dark and moving portrait of Victorian London--the untold lives of the women killed by Jack the Ripper. Polly, Annie, Elizabeth, Catherine and Mary-Jane are famous for the same thing, though they never met. They came from Fleet Street, Knightsbridge, Wolverhampton, Sweden, and Wales. They wrote ballads, ran coffee houses, lived on country estates, they breathed ink-dust from printing presses and escaped people-traffickers. They died because they were in the wrong place at the wrong time--but their greatest misfortune was to be born a woman.

The Busybody Book Club is available in print, large print, eBook, and digital audiobook. To get your copy, place a hold and borrow it at your nearest Sno-Isle Libraries location. Check out the complete 2026 Mystery Lovers title list. Registration is required to receive a reminder email with the Zoom link 24 hours before the event begins. If you register less than 24 hours before the event, your confirmation email will have the Zoom link. Contact us with questions Programs & Events FAQs To request a sign language interpreter, complete this online form or contact your local library at least two weeks prior to the library event. Interpreters will be provided based on availability.

events.title: North Branch Book Club
events.description: The North Branch has been providing a book club display for quite some time, and lots of people have taken books off that display and talked with us about them. Now is the time to sit down and talk with us about the book. For the month of May, we will be reading and discussing Babel by R. F. Kuang. If you have read any of the previous books that have been on display, we would love to hear your thoughts on those too!

events.title: Book Clubbing : Book Discussion
events.description: Join your local librarians for an evening at the Jupiter Branch for a little “You-Time”. Make friends, discover new books, enjoy engaging discussions. In May we'll be reading: The Echo of Old Books by Barbara Davis. Copies are available at the Jupiter Branch.

events.title: As the Page Turns Virtual Book Club
events.description: Welcome to the As The Page Turns Book Club, a monthly book club for adults! We will read a different book each month and engage in a discussion together. There is a limit to 15 participants per meeting. Here are the books we'll be reading in 2026! More will be added as the year progresses. This is subject to change. Please register now to receive the Zoom link before our conversation. This month's book:Fun for the Whole Family

events.title: Graphic Novel Book Club: They Called Us Enemy
events.description: As part of One Book, One Coast; we'll be reading and discussing They Called Us Enemy by George Takei with illustrations by Harmony Becker for our first book club in May. You can find this book, and all future books in the graphic novel book club with no waitlist or holds, they will always be available to read digitally through our new comics database Comics Plus: https://ezproxy.krl.org/login?...

location.name: Bremerton - Dr. Martin Luther King Jr.

events.title: Celebrate AANHPI Month: Voyagers Book Club for Grades 3 and 4
events.description: Please join us if you are currently a 3rd or 4th grader! Sign up for Voyagers Book Club at the Cupertino Library Children's Reference Desk. You will receive the book to read. Come to Book Club on Thursday, May 28th at 4:00 PM to discuss the book. Book Club will end at 5:00 PM. Sign up at the children's desk beginning Monday, May 4th. Generously sponsored by the Friends of Cupertino Library.For accessibility accommodations (ex. ASL), please fill out the online form at sccld.org/accessibility 7+ days prior to the event. If you need assistance with the form, speak with a staff member at your local library, call (408) 540-3945, or email us at ask@sccl.org.

events.title: Fiction Readers Book Group at Deer Creek: The Last Ranger
events.description: Join us for our brand new monthly book group! New members are always welcome, and since we're just getting started everyone is a new member. This Month's Title: The Last Ranger

events.title: Online Rainbow Book Club
events.description: Online Rainbow Book Club is for all readers ages 18 and over who enjoy thoughtful and supportive discussions of titles centering on LGBTQ+ authors, characters, and themes. This month, we will go over All This Could Be Different by Sarah Thankam Mathews. By joining, you agree to respect your fellow participants. Register with your email on this page. You will receive information on how to access the Zoom event on the day before the program.

events.title: Book Clubs of EPL: The Dutch House
events.description: Love reading? Love to talk about books you've read? Come join us for lively discussions on all types of fiction and non-fiction. We'll feature different titles and authors each month. Come and share your impressions, thoughts and interpretations of a book we have all read. For the month of May we will be reading The Dutch House by Ann Patchett.

book.title: The Dutch House by Ann Patchett

events.title: Family Reading Club
events.description: Join the Family Reading Club for an evening of stories, connection, and fun for the whole family! No need to read ahead in this book club! Every month, we'll read aloud a story and discuss it as a family. Dig deeper with book-related activities, such as process art, STEM challenges, and more. Collect a unique button at each session! This program is geared toward elementary school students and their families, but all ages are welcome. Contact us with questions Programs & Events FAQs To request a sign language interpreter, complete this online form or contact your local library at least two weeks prior to the library event. Interpreters will be provided based on availability.

events.title: Dark Vibes: Home Sweet Haunt
events.description: An online book club focusing on the darker side of life. Genres include horror, thrillers, true crime, and noir. Choose from a selection of books that we provide every month. After you register, you can choose to receive a print copy of a title from the list at your home branch. You may also access them as ebooks via Libby. This month, we're going home, and it's terrible. The Haunting of Hill House by Shirley JacksonHell House by Richard MathesonHow to Sell A Haunted House by Grady HendrixPlay Nice by Rachel HarrisonThe Shining by Stephen KingSeptember House by Cassandra OrlandoModel Home by Rivers SolomonWhite is for Witching by Helem Oyeyemi

events.title: Milpitas Adult Book Discussion Group - The Fetishist by Katherine Min
events.description: In May, the Milpitas Library Adult Book Group will be reading The Fetishist by Katherine Min. You are invited to join in the lively discussion. May 27th at 11:00 AM In Our Program Room May 28th at 7:30 PM In Our Program Room Novel “Sinister yet tender. . . Set in the world of classical music, rife with its hierarchies and stereotypes, this taut novel interrogates grief, forgiveness, and betrayal. ”—Boston Globe Library copies are available for check out here. The Milpitas Library Book Discussion Group meets on the 4th Wednesday morning and 4th Thursday evening of each month with the exception of November and December, when we will reschedule to accommodate holiday schedules.For Adults, Ages 18+ We thank the Friends of the Milpitas Library for making this program possible.For accessibility accommodations (ex. ASL), please fill out the online form at sccld.org/accessibility 7+days prior to the event. If you need assistance with the form, speak with a staff member at your local library, call (408) 540-3945, or email us at ask@lib.sccgov.org.

events.title: Little Makers Club (Ages 0-5) - Spring!
events.description: This themed, literacy-based drop in program is chock full of activities to help prepare your child for school and beyond. We'll work on motor skills, literacy and counting all while playing in an enriching environment that allows for socialization and for each child to choose their own activity (matching their energy level). Crafts and toddler-friendly snacks will be provided, along with a weekly take-home goody bag to continue learning at home. Manage your event registration through "My Events" when signed in to a library account. DISCOVER MORE Running of out books to read? Ask a librarian for even more personalized recommendations!Visit the Kids Page to discover additional recommendations and resources.

events.title: Silent Book Club
events.description: Join us for an hour of peaceful, quiet reading in nature at Historic Smithville Park - Mansion Rose Garden ! All are welcome, as this club doesn’t involve membership or required reading. Attendees can bring whatever they’re currently reading, or choose from a selection of books we'll bring along from the library’s collection. Also, attendees are encouraged to bring their own comfortable seating or a blanket, as well as a picnic-style snack or lunch. We’ll begin by settling in and sharing about what we’re reading. Then, we’ll start the timer for one hour of silent reading. When the timer ends, everyone is free to continue reading, chat with each other or enjoy the park. Registration is recommended, but not required. Attendees can park in the lots accessible from Meade Lane, and then proceed to the rose garden behind the mansion walls. This program is presented by staff in collaboration between the Burlington County Library System and the Burlington County Parks System.This program is presented by library staff.

events.title: Non-fiction Book Club
events.description: Discuss biographies, histories, memoirs, true crime, and other non-fiction genre titles in the Non-fiction Book Club. May's book is Uncanny Valley by Anna Weiner.

events.title: Central Book Club
events.description: Looking for your next favourite read? Join our Central Book Club to read and discuss great titles! New members welcome. In May, we'll read Real Americans by Rachel Khong and in June, we'll read The Art Thief by Michael Finkel. Note: Please register for each month separately.

events.title: Book Clubs of EPL: The Apollo Murders
events.description: Love reading? Love to talk about books you've read? Come join us for lively discussions on all types of fiction and non-fiction. We'll feature different titles and authors each month. Come and share your impressions, thoughts and interpretations of a book we have all read. This week we will be reading The Apollo Murders by Chris Hadfield."From internationally bestselling astronaut Chris Hadfield comes an exceptional Cold War thriller from the dark heart of the Space Race. As Russian and American crews sprint for a secret bounty hidden away on the Moon's surface, old rivalries blossom and the political stakes are stretched to breaking point back on Earth. Houston flight controller Kazimieras 'Kaz' Zemeckis must do all he can to keep the NASA crew together, while staying one step ahead of his Soviet rivals. But not everyone on board Apollo 18 is quite who they appear to be"

events.title: Conversations about Death and Dying Book Club
events.description: Join us for a conversation about books focusing on the inevitable yet compelling topic of death and dying. Bring your curiosity, stories, and thoughts as we explore literary experiences and memoirs on how to make the most of our finite lives. Note: This is not a counselling session or bereavement support group. This month, we will be reading Reimagining Death by Lucinda Herring. After registering for this event, please check your email for instructions on how to get your copy to read.For all those seeking to reclaim their innate and legal right to care for their own dead, create home funeral vigils, and choose greener after-death care options that are less toxic and more sustainable for the earthMore natural after-death care options are transforming the paradigm of the existing funeral industry, helping families and communities recover their instinctive capacity to care for a loved one after death and do so in creative, nourishing, and healing ways. In reclaiming these practices and creating new, innovative options, we are greening the gateway of death and returning home to ourselves, our bodies, and the earth. Lucinda Herring reminds us of the sacredness of death itself; her compelling stories, poetry, and guidance come from years of experience as a home funeral/green burial consultant and licensed funeral director dedicated to more natural and healing death practices. In Reimagining Death she shares with readers her experience caring for her own mother after death. Through storytelling and resources Herring also reveals to families the gifts of partnering with nature, home funeral vigils, sacred care at death, conscious dying (through the story of a Death with Dignity with accompanying photos of one man's planned death and after-death care), bringing laughter and a greater lightness of being to death, natural burials, and emerging eco-conscous dispositions. A valuable resource in planning for all deaths in all circumstances (with a chapter on what to do when a death occurs outside of the home), this book also guides readers on how to create an advance after-death care directive.

events.title: Silent Book Club
events.description: Join us at Riverton Library for an hour of peaceful, quiet reading! There is no membership or required reading for this club. All are welcome to bring whatever you are currently reading or check out a book from our collection. For the first 15 minutes as everyone arrives, we will find a seat, get comfortable and chat about what we are reading before our organizer starts our one-hour timer. Feel free to bring your own coffee, tea or snacks. When the timer is up, you are free to keep reading or chat with other attendees. This program is presented by library staff.

events.title: Noname Book Club
events.description: Noname Book Club is a Black-owned worker cooperative connecting community members both inside and outside carceral facilities with radical books. Each month, we uplift two books written by Black, indigenous, and other people of color. We believe building community through political education is crucial for our liberation. We also believe everyone (especially racialized and colonized people) should have access to unlimited educational materials. This is why we make sure all the resources we offer are free. Stay tuned for our next book club monthly selections! Get accessibility information.

events.title: Snack Chat: Harry Potter and the Chamber of Secrets
events.description: Journey through Hogwarts in our monthly Harry Potter Snack Chat book club. We’ll chat about magical moments, friendship, and favorite chapters while enjoying snacks. Ages 8-17. This month's book is "Harry Potter and the Chamber of Secrets" by J.K. Rowling. Available as an eBook and audiobook in the cloudLibrary app.

events.title: Fan Art Book Club
events.description: Do you have a book you loved so much you just had to make art about it? Come to Fan Art Book Club! You can either bring in art you've done or make the fan art right in the program! Everyone will chat about their books and art and ask questions. Snacks will be provided. Ages 10 -17.

events.title: Book Clubs of EPL: Talking to Strangers
events.description: Love reading? Love to talk about books you've read? Come join us for lively discussions on all types of fiction and non-fiction. We'll feature different titles and authors each month. Come and share your impressions, thoughts and interpretations of a book we have all read. This month we will be reading Talking to Strangers by Malcolm Gladwell.Malcolm Gladwell, host of the podcast 'Revisionist History' and best-selling author of 'The Tipping Point', 'Outliers', and 'What the Dog Saw', offers a powerful examination of our interactions with strangers and why they often go wrong.Please login to your account with your library card and then register to reserve a copy of the book.

events.title: One Book, One Coast: George Takei
events.description: Registration is required. Register here. This free livestream event is produced by LA County Libraries.LA County Librarian and Director, Dr. Skye Patrick and Long Beach Public Library Director, Cathy de Leon will appear in conversation with actor, author, and activist George Takei in celebration of One Book, One Coast. The largest book club on the West Coast wraps up with a special event with George Takei, author of “They Called Us Enemy.” The discussion will explore the experiences that led to the creation of his memoir, what it means to be a good neighbor and citizen in the face of overwhelming challenges, and how shared books and stories build stronger communities. George Takei is a renowned civil rights activist, social media icon, Grammy-nominated recording artist, New York Times bestselling author, and pioneering actor whose career has spanned over six decades. Best known for his role as Hikaru Sulu in “Star Trek,” Takei has appeared in more than 40 feature films and hundreds of television episodes. He has leveraged his public profile to champion social justice, LGBTQ+ rights, and marriage equality. Takei’s advocacy is deeply personal. As a child during World War II, he and his family were among the 125,000 Japanese Americans unjustly imprisoned in U.S. incarceration camps—a formative experience that shaped his lifelong commitment to civil rights and public service. Currently, Takei serves as chair emeritus and is a member of the Board of Trustees of the Japanese American National Museum. He was appointed to the Japan-United States Friendship Commission by President Bill Clinton and, in 2004, was honored by the Emperor of Japan with the Order of the Rising Sun, Gold Rays with Rosette, for his significant contributions to U.S.-Japan relations. Thanks to our partner LA County Libraries.

events.title: One Book, One Coast: They Called Us Enemy
events.description: One Book, One Coast (OBOC) is a multi-state community reading initiative that invites readers to engage with a shared text, the inaugural selection is a graphic memoir titled "They Called Us Enemy" print version link by Geroge Takei that recounts his childhood experience of incarceration alongside more than 120,000 people of Japanese descent, most of whom were U.S. citizens, following Executive Order 9066 in 1942. Register to view the livestream from anywhere, after registering at this link: https://library-lacounty-gov.z...

events.title: Spice of the Season
events.description: Do you love to cook and want to try new flavors and recipes? It’s time to get spicy and join the Spice of the Season Club! Each season we will feature a new spice and make it available in a take-home kit. Spice kits will be available the first Monday of the quarter (Jan, April, July and Oct). The kits will include a small sample of the spice, a brochure with the history of the spice, recipes from the JCPL collection, book recommendations and more. Additional recipes will be provided for each seasonal spice in the months between each spice drop. Spice kits will be available on the first Monday of each quarter, with a limited number available. ¿Te encanta cocinar y quisieras probar nuevos sabores y recetas? Participa en nuestro club del Sabor de la Temporada. Cada temporada tendremos una especie disponible para llevar a casa en un kit preparado. Los kits de especie estarán disponible el primer lunes de cada trimestre (enero, abril, julio y octubre.) Los kits incluyen una muestra de la especia, un folleto con información histórica de la especie, recetas, recomendaciones y más. Tendremos recetas adicionales para recoger en los meses entre el cambio de especie. Los kits de especia serán disponibles el primer lunes de cada temporada. Cantidades de kits disponibles serán limitadas.

events.title: Monday Morning Book Club
events.description: The Monday Morning Book Club meets from September to June at the Main Library in the New Books Section on the Main Floor at 10:00 a.m. Please contact book club host, Amy Lychwa, for details. Please note that we have a limited number of books available. As such, copies will be made available to attending members after the meeting and are given on a first come first served basis for registered members. Any remaining books will be set aside at the Main Library Circulation desk and will be available to check out. The due date for all book club selections is the next month's meeting date. The timely return of books ensures that book clubs can run smoothly and is an important part of the process. By participating in this program, you acknowledge that you accept and agree to the Guelph Public Library Code of Conduct.Accessible programs and events: Sensory kits are available by request at all library programs. Please let us know a few days before a program if you need any accessibility accommodations so we can plan for you. For sign language interpreters we ask for a minimum of two weeks’ notice. When registering for a program with a support person, please include them as an additional guest. Alternative formats are available as per the Accessibility per Ontarians Act by contacting Library Communications at 519-824-6220.

events.title: Edmonds Library Book Group - "The Lonely Hearts Book Club" by Lucy  Gilmore
events.description: Welcome to the Edmonds Library Book Group! This month we will be discussing The Lonely Hearts Book Club by Lucy Gilmore. "A young librarian and an old curmudgeon forge the unlikeliest of friendships in this charming uplifting novel about one misfit book club and the lives it changed along the way." Physical books can be picked up at the front desk in the previous month. eBooks and audiobooks can be accessed through Libby or Overdrive. If you haven't finished the book by the meeting date and would like to join, you are more than welcome. Beware, spoilers ahead. Contact us with questions Programs & Events FAQs To request a sign language interpreter, complete this online form or contact your local library at least two weeks prior to the library event. Interpreters will be provided based on availability.

events.title: Monday Book Club
events.description: You're invited to attend the Pleasant Hill Library's Monday book club. This book club will meet on June 1 at 1:00 pm; where we will be discussing Martyr! by Kaveh Akbar. Copies of the book will be available for pickup at the Pleasant Hill Library starting 5/4/26.

events.title: Children's Book Reading (Persian)
events.description: Join Sara Mohajeri from Kidswonder ( شگفتی های کودکانه ) for engaging Farsi book readings and building language and literacy skills in a welcoming and safe space. For kids ages 3-9.

events.title: Online Teen Book Club
events.description: Join our online Teen Book Club and connect with other readers. Each month on Monday evenings, we’ll explore a new title ranging from bestselling teen books to graphic novels. Share your thoughts, ask questions and dive into a thrilling story. A staff member will lead the discussion on Zoom. Johnson County participants will receive a copy about a month before the meeting. Registration is required. Upcoming titles: June – Nimona by ND Stevenson

events.title: Readability Book Group for Adults with Disabilities
events.description: The Readability Book Group is a group for adults with disabilities that is run in partnership with Center for Independent Futures. We meet weekly on Mondays via Zoom. We read a new book (or books!) every 3 months. Our sessions run from January-March, April-June, July-September and October-December. Each meeting, we will recap what we have read so far, then the facilitator will read the next part of the book aloud. Afterwards, we will discuss what we just read. Our book for April-June will be: THE BOOK WITCH by Meg Shaffer. Registration can be done here on the library website or via Center for Independent Futures. Note: There will be no book group on May 25th

events.description: Join fellow manga enthusiasts for our Monthly Manga Club—just for adults! We’ll explore a different manga series and gather to discuss the art, characters, plot twists, and themes. Whether you're a longtime fan or new to the genre, this is a fun, casual space to share your thoughts and discover new titles. No registration required—just read the selected title beforehand and drop in! In June we'll be discussing: Sasaki and Miyano by Sho, Harusono

Read any book that falls into the monthly theme and get ready to share and receive great book recommendations! Held at: Esters Gold's Marketplace, 10151 W 26th Ave, Wheat Ridge, CO 80215Please leave four-legged friends at home. Service animals are welcome. This Month's Theme: Western Settlement

events.title: Ygnacio Valley Library Book Club
events.description: Join the Ygnacio Valley Library Book Group for lively, thoughtful book discussions! Share your thoughts and connect with fellow readers. events.title: Point Reyes Book Club: What We Can Know
events.description: The Point Reyes Library Book Club will discuss What We Can Know by Ian McEwan on Monday, June 1 from 7 to 8 pm. This book club will meet online. Facilitator - Lorelei Sontag Contact: Mattie Ivy Leeds at 415-663-8375 or mattie.ivyleeds@marincounty.gov to register, or for more information. About the Book: What We Can Know by Ian McEwan"2014: At a dinner for close friends and colleagues, renowned poet Francis Blundy honors his wife's birthday by reading aloud a new poem dedicated to her, 'A Corona for Vivien'. Much wine is drunk as the guests listen, and a delicious meal consumed. Little does anyone gathered around the candlelit table know that for generations to come people will speculate about the message of this poem, a copy of which has never been found, and which remains an enduring mystery. 2119: Just over one hundred years in the future, much of the western world has beensubmerged by rising seas following a catastrophic nuclear accident. Those who survive are haunted by the richness of the world that has beenlost. In the water-logged south of what used to be England, Thomas Metcalfe, a lonely scholar and researcher, longs for the early twenty-first century as he chases the ghost of one poem, 'A Corona for Vivian'. How wild and full of risk their lives were, thinks Thomas, as he pores over the archives of that distant era, captivated by the freedoms and possibilities of human life at its zenith. When he stumbles across a clue that may lead to the elusive poem's discovery, a story is revealed of entangled loves and a brutal crime that destroy his assumptions about people he thought he knew intimately well." ADA Accommodation Request | Solicitud de Adaptación ADA

events.title: Book Clubs of EPL: Convenience Store Woman
events.description: Love reading? Love to talk about books you've read? Come join us for lively discussions on all types of fiction and non-fiction. We'll feature different titles and authors each month. Come and share your impressions, thoughts and interpretations of a book we have all read. This month we will be reading: Convenience Store Woman by Sayaka MurataConvenience Store Woman is the heartwarming and surprising story of thirty-six-year-old Tokyo resident Keiko Furukura. Keiko has never fit in, neither in her family, nor in school, but when at the age of eighteen she begins working at the Hiiromachi branch of "Smile Mart," she finds peace and purpose in her life. In the store, unlike anywhere else, she understands the rules of social interaction--many are laid out line by line in the store's manual--and she does her best to copy the dress, mannerisms, and speech of her colleagues, playing the part of a "normal" person excellently, more or less. Managers come and go, but Keiko stays at the store for eighteen years. It's almost hard to tell where the store ends and she begins. Keiko is very happy, but the people close to her, from her family to her coworkers, increasingly pressure her to find a husband, and to start a proper career, prompting her to take desperate action...

book.title: Convenience Store Woman by Sayaka Murata

events.title: One Village, One Book
events.description: Our book club meets monthly at the library to discuss books set in Oakland and the Bay Area or by Bay Area authors. We select our books from a variety of genres and eras so you’re sure to find something you’re interested in. We’ll get to learn more about our city, meet some neighbors and discuss what we’ve read. This year we’re mostly alternating fiction and non-fiction books. Meetings are the first Tuesday of the month (note the change from 2025!) from 6:30-7:30pm. Come to one discussion, a few or all of them! No pre-registration or commitment is required – all we ask is that you’ve read at least some of the book and are ready to discuss it. Here’s our 2026 schedule: (Quoted descriptions are from the library catalog unless otherwise noted.) January 6, 2026: Sourdough by Robin Sloan (fiction)We’re kicking off the year with a quick read about the very Bay Area intersection of tech and food culture. Lois is an exhausted software engineer who is gifted a very special sourdough starter by her favorite bakers when they must flee the country. Pretty soon she’s part of a mysterious underground food scene at the former Alameda Naval Air Station. February 3, 2026: Family Style: Memories of An American From Vietnam by Thien Pham (graphic memoir)The book that got the most votes from our members on our year-end poll! “Told through the lens of meaningful food and meals, this graphic novel chronicles the author’s childhood immigration to America, where food takes on new meaning as he and his family search for belonging, for happiness and for the American dream.” Pham is a graphic novelist, comic artist and educator based in Oakland. March 3, 2026: Deep Care: The Radical Activists Who Provided Abortions, Defied the Law, and Fought to Keep Clinics Open by Angela Hume (non-fiction)“The story of the radical feminist networks who worked outside the law to defend abortion. Deep Care follows generations of activists and clinicians who orbited the Women’s Choice clinic in Oakland from the early 1970s until 2010, as they worked underground and above ground, in small cells and broad coalitions and across political movements with grit, conviction, and allegiances of great trust to do what they believed needed to be done–despite the law, when required.” April 7, 2026: Moonglow by Michael Chabon (fiction)“A man bears witness to his grandfather’s deathbed confessions, which reveal his family’s long-buried history and his involvement in a mail-order novelty company, World War II and the space program.” Most of it isn’t set in Oakland, but it is the only book on this list that specifically mentions Montclair Library! May 5, 2026: Automatic Noodle by Annalee Newitz (science fiction)Our May read is a slim 163 pages, but packed with interesting ideas. This book is super popular as of December 2025, so you might want to get your hold in early. “In this semicozy tale set in a dystopian near future, robots open their own restaurant, build an unusual found family and achieve personal growth. A few years after the war that led to California’s secession from the United States, four robots awake in the flooded San Francisco takeout place where they were contracted to work.” (Kirkus) “Emboldened by newly granted civil rights from the California government, the bots reboot their lives in classic Bay Area form: by opening a noodle shop…In many ways, Newitz’s near-future San Francisco resembles today’s city, with not-so-subtle nods to AI anxieties, queer identity and the modern-day immigrant experience. It is, in the words of KQED’s Alexis Madrigal, ‘The most San Francisco book that has ever been or could ever have been written.’” (Leah Worthington) (Ebook listed separately) June 2, 2026: Moneyball by Michael Lewis (non-fiction)Baseball season is in full swing, which makes this the perfect time to read Michael Lewis’s nuanced exploration of “how Billy Beene, the general manager of the Oakland Athletics, used a new kind of thinking to build a successful and winning baseball team without spending enormous sums of money.” July 7, 2026: Girls Girls Girls by Shoshana von Blanckensee (fiction)In the summer of 1996, best friends (and secret girlfriends), Hannah and Sam drive from “New York, to the fabled queer paradise of San Francisco, free from the harsh gazes of their neighbors and the stifling demands of Hannah’s devout Orthodox Jewish mother. In San Francisco, they will finally be together as a real couple, out in the open, around other queer people.” But soon they’re working at a strip club, meeting new people and trying to figure out who just they are. August 4, 2026: Power Hungry: Women of the Black Panther Party and Freedom Summer and Their Fight to Feed a Movement by Suzanne Cope (non-fiction)Journey back with us to the summer of 1969 with the story of the free breakfast movement, grocery giveaways and other nurturing mutual aid programs started by the Black Panthers. “NYU writing professor Cope rescues two female activists from obscurity in this intriguing look at the role that food played in the civil rights and Black Power movements.” If you read One Crazy Summer with us in 2025, you’ll enjoy learning the backstory to the Panthers’ food program. September 1, 2026: Kasher in the Rye by Moshe Kasher (memoir)Go back to school with this “hilarious memoir about the absurdity of addiction.” “When he was a young boy, Kasher’s mother took him on a vacation to the West Coast. Well it was more like an abduction…They moved to Oakland, California. That’s where the real fun begins, in the war zone of Oakland Public Schools. Those early years read like part Augusten Burroughs, part David Sedaris, with a touch of Jim Carrol….Brutally honest and laugh-out-loud funny, Kasher…finds humor in even the most horrifying situations.” October 6, 2026: 107 Days by Kamala Harris (memoir)Hopefully by October the miles-long hold list for this book will have settled down enough for you to get a copy! “For the first time, and with surprising and revealing insights, Kamala Harris tells the story of one of the wildest and most consequential presidential campaigns in American history.” (NPR) November 3, 2026: The Giveaway: The Clay Blackburn Story by Owen Hill (crime fiction)We have something noir-ish by an Oakland author for those long November nights. “Hill’s crime fiction is whip-smart, stylish, subversive, hilarious and just dripping with East Bay color, culture and landmarks. Sweet and sour like a perfect Negroni, Hill has invented a new kind of East Bay Noir — the fog rolls in late, the golden afternoon glow somehow both luxurious and ominous in the class war that never sleeps.” (Recommendation from Tally Ho! Books via Oaklandside) December 1, 2026: Sign My Name to Freedom by Betty Reid Soskin (memoir)Reid Soskin, famous for being the oldest park ranger in the history of the National Park Service while working at the Rosie the Riveter National Park in Richmond, has been witness to a wide sweep of American history. She grew up in the Bay Area, raised four kids, ran a record store in Berkeley and more. “Betty just turned 104 and her memoir gives a fascinating look at African Americans migration, the Rosie the Riveter story, and beyond through the Civil Rights era.” (recommender) (Ebook and audiobook listed separately) « See our 2025 books here »« See our 2024 books here »« See our 2023 books here »Learn more about Montclair Library and its Friends group, FOML, here: About | Friends of Montclair Library

events.title: Intermediate ESL Book Club - Lemon Meringue Pie Murder
events.description: Carla's intermediate ESL Book Club will read Lemon Meringue Pie Murder by Joanne Fluke. This book club is best for intermediate English learners. Students will read at home each week. Then we will meet as a group once a week to talk about the story, learn new vocabulary, and practice conversation. Book club will be led by Carla Passero. Meeting dates: April 14, 21, 28; May 5, 12, 19, 26; June 2, 23, 30; July 7, 14. (No class June 9 and June 16).Meeting time: Tuesday evenings 7pm - 8pmWhere: CCC Room next to Member Services DeskLed by: Carla PasseroRegistration is open now! Book club is limited to 8 participants. Books will be provided at the first meeting for registered participants to keep, If you do not show up at the first meeting and have not made arrangements with the tutor, we will give your spot to the next learner on the wait list. Please consider your schedule and do not register if you will miss more than one meeting. About the book:Hannah Swensen thought she'd finally discovered the recipe for a perfect life. But her sometime beau Norman Rhodes tosses a surprise ingredient into the mix when he phones to tell her he's just bought a house from local drugstore clerk Rhonda Scharf--which he plans to tear down in order to build the dream home he and Hannah designed. It seems the plan has been cooking for quite some time, and Hannah's shocked. Especially since her ring finger is still very much bare. . .The good news is that the soon-to-be-torn-down house is full of antiques--and Norman has given Hannah and her mother first dibs. They uncover some gorgeous old furniture, a patchwork quilt. . .and Rhonda Scharf's dead body. A little more sleuthing turns up the half-eaten remains of a very special dinner for two--and one of The Cookie Jar's famous lemon meringue pies. Now it's up to Hannah to turn up the heat--and get busy tracking down clues. Starting in her very own kitchen. . .

events.title: Better Off Read Book Club - The family recipe
events.description: A stunning family dramedy about estranged siblings competing to inherit their father's Vietnamese sandwich franchise and unravel family mysteries. Duc Tran, the eccentric founder of the Vietnamese sandwich chain Duc's Sandwiches, has decided to retire. No one has heard from his wife, Evelyn, in two decades. She abandoned the family without a trace, and clearly doesn't want anything to do with Duc, the business, or their kids. But the money has to go to someone. With the help of the shady family lawyer, Duc informs his five estranged adult children that to receive their inheritance, his four daughters must revitalize run-down shops in old-school Little Saigon locations across America: Houston, San Jose, New Orleans, and Philadelphia--within a year. But if the first-born (and only) son, Jude, gets married first, everything will go to him. Each daughter is stuck in a new city, battling gentrification, declining ethnic enclaves, and messy love lives, while struggling to modernize their father's American dream. Jude wonders if he wants to marry for love or for money--or neither. As Duc's children scramble to win their inheritance, they begin to learn the real intention behind the inheritance scheme--and the secret their mother kept tucked away in the fireplace, all along. The Family Recipe is about rediscovering one's roots, different types of fatherly love, legacy, and finding a place in a divided country where the only commonality among your neighbors is the universal love of sandwichesThe City of Evanston is committed to promoting a Citywide culture of accessibility and inclusivity. events.title: Melinda Myers Webinar: Managing Your Landscape with Pollinators in Mind
events.description: You can have a landscape that you and the pollinators will enjoy with a few changes to your garden and landscape maintenance. We’ll cover pollinator-friendly plant care to keep your containers, gardens and landscape healthy and looking its best throughout the year. Then we’ll explore ways to manage weeds, diseases and insect pests without chemicals that are harmful to your plants and visiting pollinators. Register for this FREE webinar at: https://www.melindamyers.com/about-melinda/upcoming-appearances *The webinar is sponsored by: About Melinda MyersMelinda Myers has written more than 20 gardening books, including the Midwest Gardener’s Handbook, 2nd Edition and Small Space Gardening. She hosts The Great Courses “How to Grow Anything” streaming courses and the nationally syndicated Melinda’s Garden Moment radio program. Myers is also a columnist and contributing editor for Birds & Blooms magazine and writes and nationally syndicated weekly garden column. Before her current media work, Melinda hosted “The Plant Doctor” radio show on WTMJ 620 AM for over 20 years as well as 7 seasons of “Great Lakes Gardener” on PBS. She has a master’s degree in horticulture and was a horticulture instructor with tenure. She was also recently inducted into the National Association for Garden Communicators Hall of Fame.Melinda’s website is www.MelindaMyers.com where you can find gardening videos, gardening tips and information, monthly garden checklists, and more.

events.title: Silent Book Club
events.description: Do you find it difficult to find time to read? Do you hate reading assigned books? Are you anxious in a traditional book club setting? If you answered yes to any of these, then check out Silent Book Club. A new type of book club, where there's no assigned reading and all readers are welcome.BYOBook (print, eBooks, audiobooks, textbooks, comic books, etc.) and settle in for an hour of silent, sustained reading. At the end of the hour, attendees can discuss what they're reading—or not! A fun way to hear about what others are reading and share what you're reading. events.title: High Intermediate - Advanced ESL Book Club - A Farewell to Arms
events.description: ESL Book Club will read A Farewell to Arms by Ernest Hemingway. This book club is best for high intermediate - advanced English learners. Students will read at home each week. Then you will meet as a group once a week to talk about the story, learn new vocabulary, and practice conversation. Book club will be led by Fernando Lopez. Meeting dates: May 28; June 4, 11, 18, 25; July 2, 9, 16.Meeting time: Thursdays, 10:30am - 12:00nRegistration is open now! Book club is limited to 8 participants. Books will be provided for registered participants to keep. Please consider your schedule and do not register if you will miss more than one meeting.A Farewell to Arms:Hemingway’s masterpiece is not only one of the best novels we have about World War I but is also a tender, haunting love story. Ernest Hemingway drew from his own war experiences when he crafted this remarkable story of an American ambulance driver serving on the Italian front and his love for a beautiful English nurse. The novel, Hemingway’s first best seller, is marked by vivid depictions of the horrors of the battlefield—but also by the heartrending vicissitudes of a passionate affair of the heart between his protagonists, Frederic and Catherine, leading up to a tragic ending that is all the more powerful for its famously understated expression.

events.title: Roblox Book Club (ages 8-12)
events.description: CPL’s Roblox Book Club is a unique hybrid experience designed for 8-12-year-olds who like reading and playing Roblox. You can participate on your own time, on any day, at the library, or from the comfort of your own home—although you’ll have to come in person at some point to pick up your prize! Read on to learn more. How does it work? Log in to Roblox using your personal account, or if you’re at the library, one of our special CPL accounts.Search for CPLBLOX, our official Roblox experience.CPLBLOX will drop your player avatar into a replica of the Children's Library. Once you’re in, go to the Friends' Activity Room just like if you were attending a book club program in the physical library.Going through the portal will bring you to the Trivia Game.Use your platforming skills to climb the obstacle course, answering 10 trivia questions about the monthly book along the way. When you reach the top, you will find the secret password.Tell the password to the librarian at the (real) Children’s Desk, and they will give you your prize! Every month will feature a new book, new trivia and a new prize password. May: Efrén Divided June: The Lost Library July: TBD August: TBD

events.title: Bite Sized Book Club- Harriet Tubman: Live in Concert
events.description: Think you're too busy for a book club? Think again! Schlafly's "Bite-Sized Books" features diverse selections under 250 pages - a perfect fit for busy students, parents and professionals. Whether you're looking to try new genres, sample a different perspective or fit in a quick read during your kiddo's nap time, this club has you covered. Join us for our Saturday one-hour discussions every month. And if our current theme isn't for you, don't fret - check back in July for our next topic!This unit's focus is "Fantastical History", and June's selection is "Harriet Tubman: Live in Concert" by Bob the Drag Queen (233 pages). This read has elements of historical fiction and magical realism, and features LGBTQ+ characters. Description - "Harriet Tubman and four of the enslaved persons she led to freedom want to tell their story in a unique way - by following in the footsteps of Lin-Manuel Miranda’s 'Hamilton'."

events.title: Adv. 3D Printing for Youth
events.description: Ready to take your 3D design skills to the next level? Join us at Advanced 3D Printing for Youth! In this club, we’ll dive deeper into 3D modeling techniques, troubleshoot designs, and experiment with more advanced tools. Whether you want to create functional gadgets, detailed miniatures, or creative prints, this is your chance to push your understanding of 3D printing! EPL staff will provide guidance and feedback as you refine your designs! Each participant’s final design will be 3D printed for free and available for pickup at your local EPL branch. Recommended for ages 9-15 with prior experience in TinkerCAD or 3D design. If attendees want to create an object larger than 6cm x 6cm x 6cm, parents must complete the certification and bring a USB to the class so that the Makerspace can support with processing the print! To 3D print at the Makerspace: An adult (or youth aged 12+) must be certified to 3D print, which is a quick online process found on our website!Book the printer by calling 780-944-5342, email makerspace@epl.ca, or chat face to face with Makerspace staff!Slice the design in the Makerspace, in Children's library, in our class, or on your own devices!Bring the file on a USB to the Makerspace to process. Other branches with on-the-floor 3D printing services are: Abbottsfield, Mill Woods, and Capilano!

events.title: Book Clubs of EPL: Circe
events.description: Love reading? Love to talk about books you've read? Join our book club for spirited discussions on a wide range of fiction and non-fiction. Each month we'll tackle a new title. Whether you loved it, loathed it, or left it unfinished, we want to hear your take. Come share your impressions and connect with fellow readers! This week we will be reading Circe by Madeline MillerIn the house of Helios, god of the sun and mightiest of the Titans, a daughter is born. But Circe is a strange child -- not powerful, like her father, nor viciously alluring like her mother. Turning to the world of mortals for companionship, she discovers that she does possess power -- the power of witchcraft, which can transform rivals into monsters and menace the gods themselves.Threatened, Zeus banishes her to a deserted island, where she hones her occult craft, tames wild beasts and crosses paths with many of the most famous figures in all of mythology, including the Minotaur, Daedalus and his doomed son Icarus, the murderous Medea, and, of course, wily Odysseus.With unforgettably vivid characters, mesmerizing language, and page-turning suspense, Circe is a triumph of storytelling, an intoxicating epic of family rivalry, palace intrigue, love and loss, as well as a celebration of indomitable female strength in a man's world.

Volunteers who register but have not completed the Application Form may have their registration cancelled. Volunteers for this program will earn 2-hours of Community Service each of the 6-weeks. You will be asked to... Prepare program materialsTidy up program materials Providing 1-1 or small group support during an activity or craftRole model appropriate behaviour Registration Required. Want to know more about volunteering at the Library? Visit our website! As a Library volunteer, you are expected to... Be on time and reliable – Show up when you’re supposed to and follow through with your responsibilities.Be responsible and dedicated – Do your tasks well and take them seriously.Respect the staff and follow their lead – Listen to your supervisors and do what they ask.Dress and act appropriately – Wear the right clothes and behave in a professional way at all times.Keep things private – Don’t share personal information about patrons, staff, or other volunteers.

events.title: High Intermediate - Advanced ESL Book Club - A Farewell to Arms
events.description: ESL Book Club will read A Farewell to Arms by Ernest Hemingway. This book club is best for high intermediate - advanced English learners. Students will read at home each week. Then you will meet as a group once a week to talk about the story, learn new vocabulary, and practice conversation. Book club will be led by Fernando Lopez. Meeting dates: May 28; June 4, 11, 18, 25; July 2, 9, 16.Meeting time: Thursdays, 10:30am - 12:00nRegistration is open now! Book club is limited to 8 participants. Books will be provided for registered participants to keep. Please consider your schedule and do not register if you will miss more than one meeting.A Farewell to Arms:Hemingway’s masterpiece is not only one of the best novels we have about World War I but is also a tender, haunting love story. Ernest Hemingway drew from his own war experiences when he crafted this remarkable story of an American ambulance driver serving on the Italian front and his love for a beautiful English nurse. The novel, Hemingway’s first best seller, is marked by vivid depictions of the horrors of the battlefield—but also by the heartrending vicissitudes of a passionate affair of the heart between his protagonists, Frederic and Catherine, leading up to a tragic ending that is all the more powerful for its famously understated expression.

events.title: Book Clubs of EPL: Miss Kopp's Midnight Confessions
events.description: Love reading? Love to talk about books you've read? Come join us for lively discussions on all types of fiction and non-fiction. We'll feature different titles and authors each month. Join us to share your impressions, thoughts and interpretations of a book we have all read.Miss Kopp's Midnight Confessions by Amy Stewart SYNOPSIS: Deputy sheriff Constance Kopp is outraged to see young women brought into the Hackensack jail over dubious charges of waywardness, incorrigibility, and moral depravity. Constance uses her authority as deputy sheriff, and occasionally exceeds it, to investigate and defend these women when no one else will. But it's her sister Fleurette who puts Constance's beliefs to the test and forces her to reckon with her own ideas of how a young woman should and shouldn't behave.

Accompanied by her horse, Tarzan, and her dog, Depeche Toi, she navigated the changing landscape of the 1950s, relying on the kindness of strangers and her own grit to complete her last-chance adventure."Note that Moraga Book Club is for adults ages 18 and up.

events.title: Teen Advisory Board @ Main TeenZone
events.description: Interested in creating your own teen programs or events? Got a club you've always wanted to start or a skill you'd love to share? Have an eye for design and ideas for book displays or holiday celebrations? Drop in to our monthly Teen Advisory Board (TAB) meetings on the second *Friday* of the month at the Main Library TeenZone to learn more about what's possible at TAB and start collaborating with other teens. Snacks provided!TAB is a youth-led, librarian-supported volunteer group for youth in 7th—12th grades who are excited to take an active role in shaping what we offer to teens at the Main TeenZone. As a TAB member, you get the opportunity to: Make real your ideas for the TeenZone as a place for teens, by teens! Develop real-world skills and experience that you can use in job and college applications Fulfill your community service hour requirements Enjoy food, fun, and friendship at our monthly meet-ups Fill out a volunteer application here. events.description: Create an account on the READsquared app or at oshlib.readsquared.com. Register online or at any branch and receive a free tracker and stickers. Group registrations are available for summer camps and daycares. Register between June 13 and June 20 for your chance to win one of eight exciting prizes! Track your reading and complete at least one activity each week: Give a book report – 30 points and a prize from the Treasure chest! (report your reading minutes as often as you like, but prizes and points are awarded once per week.) Complete a TDSRC Weekly Activity – 30 points Attend a program – 30 points Read a book from a SRC book list – 20 points Solve an online jigsaw puzzle – 10 points Answer a trivia question – 10 points Repeat. Keep reading, tracking, and completing missions. Have fun! Earn 1,000 points for a chance to win a special prize!

events.title: Adult Summer Reading Challenge

Register in person at any branch to receive summer reading swag. Let us know what you’re reading this summer by tagging us @OshawaLibraries. Create an account on the READsquared app or visit oshlib.readsquared.com Track your reading and complete challenges Repeat. Keep reading, tracking, and completing reading challenges. Have fun!

events.title: Camano Island Library Book Group - "The Measure"
events.description: Join us to celebrate the love of reading with others in our community! This month we discuss The Measure by Nikki Erlick. One day eight people open their front door to find a small wooden box. The contents of this mysterious box tells them the exact number of years they will live. As society comes together and pulls apart, everyone faces the same shocking choice: Do they wish to know how long they’ll live? And, if so, what will they do with that knowledge? This compelling book club fiction explores that very question. A limited number of copies of the book will be available at the Camano Island Library. events.description: Have an appetite for science fiction but don't have time to delve into another space opera? Join us every third Monday of the month to discuss the novellas and short story collections of award-winning sci-fi authors. Through this Spring's offerings of stories, readers will explore a range of topics, from androids to first contact, and from post-apocalyptic to Afrofuturism. Registered participants are welcome to pick up their copies of this month’s book at Divoll.For June, we’re reading The Mimicking of Known Successes, Malka Older's Sapphic sci-fi take on the Holmesian mystery genre. events.title: Manchester Book Group
events.description: Pick up the next book club selection at the Manchester library service desk following the previous month's meeting on the 3rd Monday of the month. **NOTE Meetings in January and February each year are held on the FOURTH Monday of the month due to the Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. Day and President's Day holidays This month we are discussing:The Familiar by Leigh BardugoFrom the Publisher:In a shabby house, on a shabby street, in the new capital of Madrid, Luzia Cotado uses scraps of magic to get through her days of endless toil as a scullion. But when her scheming mistress discovers the lump of a servant cowering in the kitchen is actually hiding a talent for little miracles, she demands Luzia use those gifts to improve the family's social position.What begins as simple amusement for the nobility takes a perilous turn when Luzia garners the notice of Antonio Pérez, the disgraced secretary to Spain's king. Still reeling from the defeat of his armada, the king is desperate for any advantage in the war against England's heretic queen—and Pérez will stop at nothing to regain the king's favor.Determined to seize this one chance to better her fortunes, Luzia plunges into a world of seers and alchemists, holy men and hucksters, where the lines between magic, science, and fraud are never certain. But as her notoriety grows, so does the danger that her Jewish blood will doom her to the Inquisition's wrath. She will have to use every bit of her wit and will to survive—even if that means enlisting the help of Guillén Santángel, an embittered immortal familiar whose own secrets could prove deadly for them both.

events.title: Swasey Book Club
events.description: Join us for the Swasey Book Club! June's selection is Framed by John Grisham and Jim McCloskey. About the book:"In his first work of nonfiction since The Innocent Man, #1 bestselling author John Grisham and Centurion Ministries Founder Jim McCloskey share ten harrowing true stories of wrongful convictions. Impeccably researched and grippingly told, Framed offers an inside look at the victims of the United States criminal justice system. A fundamental principle of our legal system is a presumption of innocence, but once someone has been found guilty there is very little room to prove doubt. Framed shares ten true stories of men who were innocent but found guilty and forced to sacrifice friends, families, wives, and decades of their lives to prison while the guilty parties remained free. In each of the stories, John Grisham and Jim McCloskey recount the dramatic hard-fought battles for exoneration." Stop into the Swasey branch to secure your book club copy and then join us for the discussion on June 16. We hope you'll join us! The Swasey Book Club selection checks out on a first-come, first-served basis. Supply is limited. Ask library staff for assistance. Call us at (253)280-2970 if you have any questions.

events.title: Cookbook Book Club
events.description: Do you love books and food? Are you looking for a fun, low-cost way to increase your cooking skills, try new recipes and make some new foodie friends? Then perhaps our new "Cookbook Book Club" is for you!Our book club hostess is Chef JoEllen Hothem. She is a self-educated home chef who began exploring food ways and cooking as a child through the "Mud Pies and Other Recipes" by Marjorie Winslow. She loves to learn about world cuisines through reading and travel. A firm believer in the tremendous health benefits of intentional, healthful eating, in community, she combines her love of food with her public speaking and instructional training to help people learn the principles of taste, flavor and foundational cooking skills, so they can confidently prepare tasty and healthful meals for themselves, family and friends.

events.title: The History Buffet Book Club - Salt: A World History
events.description: Get ready for a book club meeting you won't want to miss! The History Buffet Book Club will gathering at Salt + Smoke to discuss Mark Kurlansky's fascinating book, Salt: A World History. It's the perfect setting to talk about this epic story of salt's role in everything from trade and war to religion and currency. As we dive into the book's rich history, we'll also be enjoying the very thing the restaurant does best—perfectly smoked, seasoned meats. Come hungry for both knowledge and great food!

events.title: Series Sleuths Book Club
events.description: Every other week we'll meet after Summer Lunch to discuss a super sleuth book series! Join us for a themed activity, and to learn more about the great children's mystery series we have at the library. Sign up and borrow the chosen titles any time, starting April 20, at the Covington Children's Desk. June 3- Greenglass House by Kate Milford June 17- Mystery in Rocky Mountain National Park by Aaron Johnson July 1- Book Scavenger by Jennifer Chambliss Bertman July 15- The Buried Bones Mystery by Sharon M. Draper

events.title: Book Club Discussion -  Yellowface
events.description: Want to connect with others over the love of reading? Join our book club to discuss R.F. Kuang's Yellowface. Please see a staff member for details on borrowing the title.Book Description: White lies. Dark humor. Deadly consequences… Bestselling sensation Juniper Song is not who she says she is, she didn’t write the book she claims she wrote, and she is most certainly not Asian American—in this chilling and hilariously cutting novel from R.F. Kuang, the #1 New York Times bestselling author of Babel.

events.title: Volunteer Opportunity: Early Readers Book Club
events.description: Are you a High School Student looking for Community Service Hours? Register today to help out at Early Readers Book Club, a program for children 4-6 years old. All volunteers must complete the Community Service Application and Contract Form prior to volunteering at the Library. Volunteers who register but have not completed the Application Form may have their registration cancelled. Volunteers for this program will earn 2-hours of Community Service each of the 6-weeks. You will be asked to... Prepare program materialsTidy up program materials Providing 1-1 or small group support during an activity or craftRole model appropriate behaviour Registration Required. Want to know more about volunteering at the Library? Visit our website! As a Library volunteer, you are expected to... Be on time and reliable – Show up when you’re supposed to and follow through with your responsibilities.Be responsible and dedicated – Do your tasks well and take them seriously.Respect the staff and follow their lead – Listen to your supervisors and do what they ask.Dress and act appropriately – Wear the right clothes and behave in a professional way at all times.Keep things private – Don’t share personal information about patrons, staff, or other volunteers.

events.title: Early Readers Book Club
events.description: Ravenous readers and burgeoning booklovers, this is the place for you! Discover picture book authors you’ll love and build early literacy skills through interactive storytelling and activities perfect for emergent readers! Ages 4-6 | Free!Registration Required. Registration is per child. Note: Children under 12 must be supervised.Parents and caregivers are required to remain in the building and be responsible for their children at all times – including during programs. Thank you for helping us keep the Library safe and welcoming for everyone!

events.title: AI Unplugged: Discussion & Discovery
events.description: Curious about artificial intelligence (AI)? Love thoughtful conversations and big questions? This discussion group offers a relaxed, welcoming space to explore AI through hands-on experimentation, current news, and conversation.We’ll try out generative AI platforms, discuss how AI is being used (and misused), and unpack recent AI headlines. Together we’ll explore the risks, ethics, applications, and challenges of AI as we learn along the way. This is a shared learning space—no prior knowledge needed. Just bring your questions, curiosity, and a willingness to join the conversation about how AI is shaping our world. By participating in this program, you acknowledge that you accept and agree to Guelph Public Library's Code of Conduct. Alternative formats are available as per the Accessibility per Ontarians Act by contacting Library Communications at 519-824-6220. Accessible programs and events Sensory kits are available by request at all library programs.Please let us know a few days before a program if you need any accessibility accommodations so we can plan for you. For sign language interpreters we ask for a minimum of two weeks’ notice.When registering for a program with a support person, please include them as an additional guest.

events.title: Unplugged Book Club
events.description: The Unplugged Book Club is a collaborative book discussion series between ODC Network and Herrick District Library. Throughout 2026, we invite you to read books related to disconnecting from unhealthy tech habits and reconnecting with our community and nature. Want to meet some of the authors? Join the ODC Network's Unplugged: Disconnect to Reconnect speaker series to meet Catherine Price (January 14), Katherine Martinko (February 12), Dr. Scott Sampson (March 3), and Lenore Skenazy (March 17).About the Unplugged: Disconnect to Reconnect speaker series - ODC Network invites the West Michigan community to disconnect from overuse of technology and reconnect with nature and each other, while reintroducing the simple idea that outdoor and community connection is central to thriving people, thriving families, and thriving communities. Learn more at outdoordiscovery.org/unplugged Here is our Unplugged Book Club lineup: June 17th, 6:30-7:30p at DeGraaf Nature Center (600 Graafschap Rd) – How to Know a Person by David Brooks ___ July 13th, 6:30-7:30p at Herrick Main (classroom) – A Walk in the Woods by Bill Bryson ___ August 13th, 10:00-11:00a Herrick Main (classroom) – Digital Minimalism by Cal Newport ___ September 17th, 6:30- 7:30p at Herrick North Branch – The Nature Fix by Florence Williams ___ October 7th, 6:00-7:00p at Herrick Main (classroom) – My Side of the Mountain by Jean Craighead George (All ages, with Youth Services) ___ November 10th, 6:00-7:00p at Herrick Main (classroom) – Wild by Cheryl Strayed

events.title: Main Book Club
events.description: Join the Main Book Club in the Olympic Room to discuss the novel, Last Murder at the End of the World by Stuart Turton.About the book: Solve the murder to save what's left of the world.Outside the island there is nothing: the world was destroyed by a fog that swept the planet, killing anyone it touched.On the island: it is idyllic. One hundred and twenty-two villagers and three scientists, living in peaceful harmony. The villagers are content to fish, farm and feast, to obey their nightly curfew, to do what they're told by the scientists.From the bestselling author of The 7 1/2 Deaths of Evelyn Hardcastle and The Devil and the Dark Water comes an inventive, high-concept murder mystery: an ingenious puzzle, an extraordinary backdrop, and an audacious solution. Copies of The Last Murder at the End of the World are available at Main while they last for sign out, or from various branch collections.

Join other adults learning English as a Second Language in this fun and engaging club. You'll read one book a month and then come discuss it with the rest of the club. Be ready to read your favorite passages to the group! If the books on this list look too easy, try our Advanced ESL Book Club. Here's the reading list: January: Ghost Boys February: The House on Mango Street March: Come on in April: Soul Lanterns May: The Breadwinner June: Paper Wishes July: The Lost Library August: The Birchbark House September: Rosa Parks, My Story October: The Elephant Girl November: A Night Divided December: The One and Only Ivan

events.title: High Intermediate - Advanced ESL Book Club - A Farewell to Arms
events.description: ESL Book Club will read A Farewell to Arms by Ernest Hemingway. This book club is best for high intermediate - advanced English learners. Students will read at home each week. Then you will meet as a group once a week to talk about the story, learn new vocabulary, and practice conversation. Book club will be led by Fernando Lopez. Meeting dates: May 28; June 4, 11, 18, 25; July 2, 9, 16.Meeting time: Thursdays, 10:30am - 12:00nRegistration is open now! Book club is limited to 8 participants. Books will be provided for registered participants to keep. Please consider your schedule and do not register if you will miss more than one meeting.A Farewell to Arms:Hemingway’s masterpiece is not only one of the best novels we have about World War I but is also a tender, haunting love story. Ernest Hemingway drew from his own war experiences when he crafted this remarkable story of an American ambulance driver serving on the Italian front and his love for a beautiful English nurse. The novel, Hemingway’s first best seller, is marked by vivid depictions of the horrors of the battlefield—but also by the heartrending vicissitudes of a passionate affair of the heart between his protagonists, Frederic and Catherine, leading up to a tragic ending that is all the more powerful for its famously understated expression.

events.title: Newcomer Book Discussion Group
events.description: Join us to practice your English listening, reading, and speaking skills through engaging book discussions. All levels of English are welcome! Register at bit.ly/4rFFGDS or email colguin@aurorapl.ca In partnership with Catholic Community Services of York Region.

events.title: Genre Jumpers Book Club
events.description: Read a new genre every month! This month, we delve into Literary Fiction and Epistolary Novel with Ocean Vuong’s stunning debut novel, On Earth We're Briefly Gorgeous. Written as a letter from a son to a mother who cannot read, this story unearths a family's history rooted in Vietnam and replanted in Hartford, Connecticut. Through the eyes of the narrator, Little Dog, the novel explores a traumatic past, a transformative first love, and the lyrical ways we attempt to heal across generational divides. Copies will be available at the Information Desk, and the e-book and e-audiobook versions are available on CloudLibrary. Preregister.On Earth We're Briefly Gorgeous

events.title: Beyond the Book | The Odyssey
events.description: Join us for an exploration of The Odyssey by Homer. Archaeologist Melissa Morison from Grand Valley State University will discuss daily life in the ancient Greek world and how recent archaeological discoveries have shaped our understanding of the ancient Mediterranean. For adults. This lecture will be followed by The Classics Book Club | The Odyssey, happening at the Main Library on Thursday, July 16, 2026, 7:00 – 8:30 pmBeyond the Book | The OdysseyThursday, June 18 2026, 7:00 – 8:30 pmMain Library – 111 Library St NEMás Allá del Libro | La OdiseaEl jueves, 18 de junio de 2026, 7:00 – 8:30 pmBiblioteca Principal – 111 Library St NE Únase a nosotros para una exploración de La Odisea de Homero. La arqueóloga Melissa Morison, de Grand Valley State University, hablará sobre la vida cotidiana en el mundo griego antiguo y cómo los descubrimientos arqueológicos recientes han moldeado nuestra comprensión del Mediterráneo antiguo. Para adultos. Esta conferencia será seguida por El Club de Lectura de Clásicos | La Odisea, que se llevará a cabo en la Biblioteca Principal el jueves, 16 de julio de 2026, de 7:00 a 8:30 pm

events.title: Silent Book Club
events.description: Always wanted to try a book club but don’t have the time to commit to a specific book? Welcome to Silent Book Club! There's no assigned reading. All readers are welcome—ebooks, audiobooks, textbooks, comic books... it's BYOBook. We’ll meet every month, share what we’re reading and then read silently with our fellow book worms. Socialize afterwards--or not--it’s totally up to you! No registration required.

events.description: Haven't gotten over Heated Rivalry yet? Have we got the book club for you! This month, it's book #3: Tough Guy! Ryan Price & Fabian Salah couldn't be more different. Ryan's a sensitive guy, and one of hockey's most dangerous players. Fabian's a beautiful starving artist who hates hockey. Their relationship can work, but only if they can work through the issues keeping them apart!Every month we'll be running a marathon through Rachel Reid's Game Changers book series - the inspiration for the hit TV show - in publication order! The Game Changers series is available at the library digitally and in limited physical copies. This is a virtual book club - registrants will receive a link to participate before the meeting.
